---
title: "Merging Datasets"
name: "Hazel Lutz"
date: "March 16, 2026"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: sandstone
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
---

```{r packages}
lapply(c("readr", "dplyr", "haven", "ggplot2"), library, character.only = TRUE)
# haven is for dealing with stata format
```

First, just importing from all of my CSVs.
```{r importing}
pruned_dir = "./data/pruned/"
intl_cow = read_csv(paste(pruned_dir, "intnl_filtered_cow.csv", sep=""))
nonintl_cow = read_csv(paste(pruned_dir, "nonintnl_filtered_cow.csv", sep=""))
afro = read_csv(paste(pruned_dir, "afro.csv", sep=""))
americas = read_csv(paste(pruned_dir, "americas.csv", sep=""))
```

```{r exploring_pub_op}
colnames(afro)
colnames(americas)

afro = afro %>%
	rename(
	       country = country,
	       urbrur = urbrur,
	       age = q1a,
	       country_dir = q3a,
	       economy_eval = q4a,
	       economic_improve = q5a,
	       anticipated_economy = q5b,
	       felt_safety = q7a,
	       felt_free_speech = q9a,
	       felt_free_org = q9b,
	       felt_free_voting = q9c,
	       felt_election_freedom = q14a,
	       fear_political_violence = q14c,
	       support_dem = q23,
	       felt_democracy = q31,
	       felt_dem_change = q32a,
	       anticipated_dem_change = q32b,
	       political_competition_to_violence = q33a,
	       unequality_under_law = q33e,
	       trust_pres = q37a,
	       trust_parl = q37b,
	       trust_local_govt = q37d,
	       trust_ruling_party = q37e,
	       trust_opp_party = q37f,
	       trust_poilice = q37g,
	       trust_army = q37h,
	       most_important_issue1 = q45pt1,
	       most_important_issue2 = q45pt2,
	       most_important_issue3 = q45pt3,
	       gender_violence_taken_seriously = q53b,
	       neighbors_diff_party = q87e,
	       marry_diff_party = q88a,
	       mutual_citizenship = q88b
	)
# DATE COLUMN NON EXISTENT FOR WAVE 1? ARE YOU JOKING?
```
